bytes.topic: Dependency Injection — Hilt, Koin, why DI matters on Android
bytes.title: Kotlin Dependency Injection — Hilt vs Koin
bytes.body: Dependency injection (DI) is crucial for maintaining a clean, modular, and scalable Android codebase in Kotlin. It helps reduce boilerplate code, improves testability, and simplifies maintenance. Both Hilt and Koin are popular DI libraries for Kotlin Android development. Hilt, developed by Google, provides a more seamless integration with Android components, while Koin offers a more lightweight and flexible solution. When choosing between Hilt and Koin, consider the size and complexity of your project, as well as your team's familiarity with each library.

bytes.topic: Data Persistence — Room, DataStore, SharedPreferences, file storage
bytes.title: Kotlin — Choosing the Right Data Storage for Android Apps
bytes.body: When it comes to data persistence in Kotlin for Android, you have multiple options: Room, DataStore, SharedPreferences, and file storage. Room is an ORM that provides a simple, SQL-based interface for database operations. DataStore, on the other hand, is a more recent addition, providing an abstraction layer for shared preferences and other data storage solutions. SharedPreferences and file storage are simple, but less scalable options. To choose the right one, consider the complexity of your data model and the frequency of updates.

bytes.topic: App Architecture — MVVM, MVI, clean architecture with use cases
bytes.title: Kotlin Android App Architecture: MVVM vs MVI vs Clean Architecture
bytes.body: In Kotlin Android development, choosing the right architecture is crucial for scalability and maintainability. MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel) is a popular choice, leveraging data binding and live data. MVI (Model-View-Intent) is another option, focusing on unidirectional data flow. Clean Architecture, inspired by Robert C. Martin, separates the business logic from the presentation layer. Use cases, such as `GetUserUseCase`, encapsulate the business logic. Kotlin Coroutines facilitate asynchronous programming.
